user_prompt:
Publication-quality scientific schematic for an IEEE journal paper on local LLM driving evaluation. Use implementation-faithful concepts only. Clean white background, high grayscale readability, no figure numbers or captions inside the image, thesis/journal-native academic style.

Create a left-to-right pipeline diagram with exactly three panels of similar width.

Panel 1 title: Ollama-served local models
Content rules for Panel 1:
- Show exactly four family cards: DeepSeek, Llama, Phi, Qwen
- Arrange them in a 2x2 grid
- Each family card must contain exactly two small boxes: Base and Fine-tuned
- Do not add any third box, duplicate row, placeholder box, or extra unlabeled box
- Keep the model panel compact and simple

Panel 2 title: DiLu-style decision loop
Content rules for Panel 2:
- Show a three-lane highway scene with surrounding traffic
- Show one ego vehicle in the center lane
- The text label "ego vehicle" must appear exactly once and point to that single ego vehicle
- Do not repeat the ego label anywhere else
- Show arrows for Scene Observations going to Decision Process and Bounded Driving Actions returning to the highway scene
- Include the text highway-env highway benchmark at the bottom of this panel
- Do not add extra callout boxes or dashed placeholders in this panel

Panel 3 title: Benchmark-oriented reporting pipeline
Content rules for Panel 3:
- Show exactly four stacked output boxes labeled:
  task metrics
  latency
  ranking eligibility
  invalidity reasons
- Then show one Evaluation Gate diamond
- From the gate, show two branches only:
  valid comparison path -> comparative ranking and exact eligible pair analysis
  screening and failure-analysis path
- Do not add any extra dashed box, duplicate analysis box, or alternate branch

Global constraints:
- exactly one pipeline flow from left to right
- no duplicated labels if avoidable
- no cloud deployment
- no cross-scenario content
- no merge or intersection environments
- no reward equations
- no invented metrics
- no hardware diagrams
- no decorative icons except minimal neutral symbols if necessary
- publication-ready, clean, and readable in grayscale

iterations.prompt:
Create a high-quality scientific diagram with these requirements:

VISUAL QUALITY:
- Clean white or light background (no textures or gradients)
- High contrast for readability and printing
- Professional, publication-ready appearance
- Sharp, clear lines and text
- Adequate spacing between elements to prevent crowding

TYPOGRAPHY:
- Clear, readable sans-serif fonts (Arial, Helvetica style)
- Minimum 10pt font size for all labels
- Consistent font sizes throughout
- All text horizontal or clearly readable
- No overlapping text

SCIENTIFIC STANDARDS:
- Accurate representation of concepts
- Clear labels for all components
- Include scale bars, legends, or axes where appropriate
- Use standard scientific notation and symbols
- Include units where applicable

ACCESSIBILITY:
- Colorblind-friendly color palette (use Okabe-Ito colors if using color)
- High contrast between elements
- Redundant encoding (shapes + colors, not just colors)
- Works well in grayscale

LAYOUT:
- Logical flow (left-to-right or top-to-bottom)
- Clear visual hierarchy
- Balanced composition
- Appropriate use of whitespace
- No clutter or unnecessary decorative elements

IMPORTANT - NO FIGURE NUMBERS:
- Do NOT include "Figure 1:", "Fig. 1", or any figure numbering in the image
- Do NOT add captions or titles like "Figure: ..." at the top or bottom
- Figure numbers and captions are added separately in the document/LaTeX
- The diagram should contain only the visual content itself


USER REQUEST: Publication-quality scientific schematic for an IEEE journal paper on local LLM driving evaluation. Use implementation-faithful concepts only. Clean white background, high grayscale readability, no figure numbers or captions inside the image, thesis/journal-native academic style.

Generate a publication-quality scientific diagram that meets all the guidelines above.
iterations.critique:
SCORE: 10

STRENGTHS:
- Exceptional adherence to all detailed, specific prompt instructions, including exact text labels, panel structures, and negative constraints (no extra boxes/labels).
- Clean, highly readable grayscale design with excellent alignment, consistent stroke weights, and logical visual flow, making it ideal for an IEEE journal publication.
- Perfect execution of the specific technical components requested, such as the 2x2 model grid, the 3-lane highway decision loop, and the branching evaluation gate.

ISSUES:
- None. (Note: The use of the literal "->" character sequence in the final panel precisely matches your exact text requirement, though in a final manual tweak you might prefer to replace it with a proper typographical arrow symbol if desired).

VERDICT: ACCEPTABLE

early_stop_reason: Quality score 10.0 meets threshold 8.5 for journal
